sources.label: Simply Wall St - Shibaura Mechatronics (TSE:6590) overview / June events (dividend paid Jun 4, AGM Jun 17)
Yahoo Finance Japan - 6590.T news feed (no new material releases since Jun 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new orders, guidance, or capacity news since Jun 6; today's +3.73% looks like Japan SPE sector flow, not a fundamental event, so the rank-28 selective advanced-packaging exposure case is unchanged. Small negative drift because the stock keeps rerating (+25.7% YTD) without fresh evidence, thinning residual alpha on this list.
verdicts.bull: Hybrid bonders (TFC-6700/6800) and back-end SPE leverage to HBM/chiplet advanced packaging keep compounding; FY3/26 already showed back-end-driven beats and a guidance raise, and FY3/27 logic/foundry and memory capex commentary remains constructive.
verdicts.neutral: A $2.1B small cap up ~26% YTD with no incremental news since early June; the AGM on Jun 17 and the next order disclosure are the only near-term checkpoints, so the setup is drift until then.
verdicts.bear: Less direct AI exposure than pure HBM/packaging leaders, and the post-earnings rerating has consumed much of the discovery premium; a back-end capex digestion phase or FPD-segment drag would hit a now-fuller valuation.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY3/27 Q1 results and hybrid-bonder order momentum disclosures (next checkpoint: Jun 17 AGM commentary).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation rerating without new orders; back-end SPE capex digestion compresses the multiple on a small, less-liquid name.
